Details: Bench-scale studies [2536, 2548, 2553, 2642] were able to achieve moderate removals (50 to 95 percent) of some per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFASs) by coagulation, but only by using high coagulant dosage and/or low pH. Removals at lower doses and neutral pH were more limited (40 percent or less). Sampling at full scale [1705, 1706, 1756, 2164, 2174, 2175, 2441, 2508, 2509, 2518, 2619, 2645, 2668, 2683, 2685] and pilot [2518] drinking water treatment facilities observed either no removal or inconsistent removal of PFASs by conventional treatment.
